front: Work: this play violated the classical unities, angering Cardinal Richelieu and provoking a 'war of pamphlets' from Georges de Scudéry
Work: in this play, Don Rodrigue kills Don Gormas (the father of his lover Chimène) in a duel to avenge his father Don Diègue
Work: in this play, the title character fights off a Moorish invasion and earns an honorary title
Work: in this play, Chimène forces the title character to duel her champion Don Sanche, but he spares Don Sanche's life
Work: Jean Chapelain formally condemned this play, and its author briefly withdrew from public life / entered a three-year creative silence
Work: this play is based on a Spanish play by Guillén de Castro
Work: a soliloquy in this play beginning 'My father or my betrothed? Love or honor?' was originally performed by the actor Montdory, and audiences chanted along
Work: Pridamant believes he sees his son Clindor killed, but discovers Clindor is actually an actor in a play-within-a-play
Work: in this play, the magician Alcandre conjures visions in a cave, showing Pridamant the adventures of his son Clindor
Work: Tony Kushner adapted this play in 2002 as The Illusion, in which character names switch to Calisto and Theogenes between acts
Work: this comedy ends with a monologue calling theatre 'a fief whose income is good' as actors divvy up money
Work: Richard Wilbur translated this play as The Theatre of Illusion
Work: in this tragedy, Felix and Pauline discourage the title Armenian nobleman from converting to Christianity, but themselves convert after he is martyred
Work: in this comedy, Dorante pretends to be a military hero to court Clarice but mixes up her name with Lucrèce's
Work: this comedy was adapted from a play by Juan Ruiz de Alarcón
Author: this playwright wrote Cinna, about a conspiracy to kill Augustus Caesar
Author: this playwright was commissioned to write Titus et Bérénice in competition with Racine's Bérénice
Author: this playwright wrote Three Discourses on Dramatic Poetry, arguing against rigid adherence to the Aristotelian unities
Author: this playwright produced a verse translation of The Imitation of Christ
Author: this playwright's early comedy Mélite features a protagonist who forges love letters and suffers delusions of hell
Author: this playwright collaborated with Molière and Philippe Quinault on the comedy Psyché
